content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 12, 2000

Eileen Ledgerwood
Administrative Services Officer
Tulare County Superior Court
Room 303, County Civic Center
Visalia, CA 93291

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-00-190

Dear Ms. Ledgerwood:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflicts
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Is the Tulare County Superior Court required to prepare and maintain a conflict of
interest code?

CONCLUSION

Yes. The Tulare County Superior Court is required under the Act to adopt a
conflict of interest code.

ANALYSIS

Section 87300 requires every state and local government agency, including
judicial agencies, of California to adopt and promulgate a conflict of interest code. Prior
to 1985, no procedures were in place for adoption of conflict of interest codes by judicial
agencies. Therefore, statutory amendments were enacted in 1984 (Ch. 727, 1984 Stats.,
copy enclosed) providing the exemption for judicial agencies from the financial
disclosure and disqualifications provisions of the Act. (Whittington Advice Letter, No.
A-85-185, copy attached.) The bill established procedures for the preparation and review
of conflict of interest codes for the various judicial agencies, including designation of a
code reviewing body. (Sections 82011 and 87311.5.) Therefore, a judicial agency is

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections
18109 – 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

Eileen Ledgerwood
A-00-190
Page 2

required to adopt a conflict of interest code even if there is no subordinate agency in its
organizational structure.

Among other things, a conflict of interest code must require personal financial
disclosure by those members or employees of the agency (excluding judges)² who make
or participate in making decisions which may foreseeably affect private financial
interests. The code reviewing body for the Tulare County Superior Court is the Presiding
Judge of the Court. (Section 82011(g).)

Per your request, a copy of Regulation 18730, the FPPC standard conflict of
interest code, is enclosed. This regulation may be incorporated by reference as the body
of the conflict of interest code of the Tulare County Superior Court, to which the Court
will attach a list of "designated employees" who must file disclosure reports and a
description of their disclosure requirements. A fact sheet entitled, "How to Determine
Who Should be Designated in a Conflict of Interest Code," is also enclosed.

If you have any additional questions, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Moen, PhD
Political Reform Consultant II

Enclosures

² Judges file Statements of Economic Interests pursuant to section 87200, et seq. Also see section
87500(i).

QUESTION: Is the Tulare County Superior Court required to prepare and maintain a conflict of
interest code?

CONCLUSION: Yes. The Tulare County Superior Court is required under the Act to adopt a
conflict of interest code.
